docs.title: Due Diligence
docs.text: Source: Playbook v2 EN — Ch. 7 + 8 + Appendix F

docs.title: DD Phases
docs.text: Phase When Content DD1 — Initial During screening Financial & market data gathered during Stage 2 screening DD2 — Deep Dive Before signing Full operational & legal due diligence DD3 — Pre-Signing Bank / Finnvera Alignment on covenants, loan structure, bank review

docs.title: DD2 Charter Template
docs.text:
Objective: As prospective new owners, confirm acquisition financing and verify business condition and staff motivation before final purchase.

Field Content Objective Ensure financing of the acquisition and verify the condition of the business and the motivation of the staff before the final transaction, so that the business acquisition is safe to execute. EPICs Financial verification | Legal review | Staff interviews | Tech & systems audit | Customer review | Asset inventory Risks Distance to business | Technical skill gaps in team | DD is new for the team | Time pressure Champion ___ Finance lead ___ Operations lead ___ Legal contact ___ SMART Goal By [DATE], the team has built a complete Dataroom in Drive for the bank and Finnvera, enabling the purchase agreement to be signed on [DATE].

DD Checklist: - ☐ Interview all key staff (Staff Interview Form) - ☐ Complete BusBuy 30-60-90-Day Plan (see Appendix F below) - ☐ Knowledge transfer plan agreed with seller - ☐ Bank clauses cross-checked 100% against purchase agreement (covenant alignment) - ☐ Confirm share retirement option if company holds own shares - ☐ Company Owner Role Training Template set up - ☐ Use DD Template Trello board to track all tasks

Financing structure: 80/20 — 20% own equity, rest as a loan at approx. 4–8% interest, min. 7-year repayment. Negotiate 7 years; don't accept 5 years by default.

docs.title: Chapter 8: Dataroom Requirements
docs.text: All documents must be current (within 12 months). Share with bank and Finnvera.

docs.title: Financial Documents
docs.text:
• ☐ 3 years of financial statements (full view)
  • ☐ Current year P&L and balance sheet
  • ☐ Monthly cash flow for last 12 months

docs.title: Legal Documents
docs.text:
• ☐ Articles of Association (yhtiöjärjestys)
  • ☐ Shareholder register
  • ☐ Active contracts, leases, and loans

docs.title: Operational Documents
docs.text:
• ☐ Employee list with roles and salaries
  • ☐ Customer register (B2B top 10 + total count)
  • ☐ Asset inventory

Also include: Financial Analysis Sheet, executive summary (Canva template).

docs.title: Appendix F: 30-60-90 Day Plan
docs.text:
Phase Timeframe Key Objectives Success Metrics Day 1–30 — Stabilise First month post-acquisition Meet all staff individually; shadow all key roles; map all workflows; set up bank access, payroll, insurance; brief customers on ownership change All staff retained; no customer churn; basic financials accessible Day 31–60 — Systematise Month 2 Document all processes (video + written); set up CRM and sales tracking; recruit any missing key roles; implement Askel tech stack; set up weekly reporting cadence All roles documented; CRM live; weekly reporting running Day 61–90 — Grow Month 3 Launch first B2B and B2C acquisition campaigns; set revenue targets per channel; owner can step back to 5 hrs/week; first investor/board update prepared First new customers acquired; owner-independent operations; monthly reporting to board

Note: "Boots on the ground" is non-negotiable. Book accommodation for 3–6 weeks near the business.

docs.title: Investment Committee (IC)
docs.text: Source: Playbook v2 EN — Ch. 6

docs.title: 6.1 Pre-IC Kill Questions
docs.text:
Answered by minimum 2 persons before IC is convened:

  • How could we exit in 3–5 years? → Credible exit path must exist
  • Capital gain on exit min €100K per owner? → Exit math must support it
  • Can we implement tech to speed up operations? → At least 2 concrete interventions
  • Can we run this with max 5 hrs/week? → Yes, once knowledge transfer complete

Run the Valuation Risk Scoring Tool (playbook-valuation) before the IC meeting — the Business Value formula output must be in the pre-read materials.

docs.title: 6.2 IC Process
docs.text:
1. Designate Champion(s) and partner
  2. Create Executive Summary for IC & Bank (Canva exec summary template)
  3. Send pre-read materials — mandatory before attending
  4. Champion(s) present case — one-pager first, then Q&A
  5. Deal structure overview
  6. VOTING — 3/4 partners required to move forward

docs.title: 6.3 Post-IC (Positive Vote)
docs.text:
• ☐ Final negotiation / revised offer
  • ☐ Sign Letter of Intent (LOI / Aiesopimus)
  • ☐ Prepare LOI document

docs.title: Knowledge Transfer
docs.text:
Source: Playbook v2 EN — Ch. 9

Note: This is a stub skill. The v2 EN charter template is generalized and good enough for planning. Real operational lessons from Melers are in knowledge/portfolio/melers/learnings/. Revisit and expand after ~10 acquisitions.

docs.title: Project Charter Template
docs.text:
First step: Copy the Trello Scrum KanBan wall for this acquisition and assign all tasks.

Field Content Theme Knowledge Transfer & Recruitment × 3 Project scope [X] person-days / [Y] work hours Deliverables [list key deliverables] Stakeholders New owners | Seller | Key staff | Bank contact | Accountant Constraints Distance to business | Team technical skills | Time pressure | Budget SMART Goal By [DATE], we have created for the portfolio company one (1) systematic and documented Operations Management Platform / Source of Truth that allows roles to be clearly defined and easily trained. Three (3) key roles recruited by [DATE] and onboarded by [DATE].

P.E.S.T. Risks: - Political: regulatory changes - Economic: fuel costs, FX exposure - Social: staff retention, culture fit - Technical: system migration, software adoption

Roles to recruit:

Role Responsibilities KPIs Hire by date Role 1 — Operator [tbd] [tbd] [date] Role 2 — Driver/Field [tbd] [tbd] [date] Role 3 — [tbd] [tbd] [tbd] [date]

Budget: Tracking sheet to be created — estimated under €1,000.

docs.title: Lessons Learned (from v2 EN)
docs.text:
• "Boots on the ground" is non-negotiable. Book accommodation for 3–6 weeks near the business.
  • Set up the Charter document immediately — assign every task to Trello. No email management.
  • File for expedited PRH owner documentation — unblocks bank credit limit faster.
  • Share your roadmap with old owners early. They need to know WHEN things happen.
  • Set Mon/Wed/Fri as standing planning meetings.
  • You have to map key role workflows. Old owners couldn't systemise — that's why you're buying.

For Melers-specific learnings (the "I wish I had known" table, killer skills stack), see knowledge/portfolio/melers/learnings/.

docs.title: Mapping Key Role Workflows
docs.text:
• Create a workflow/process mapping system for every key role
  • Capture KPIs that measure role success
  • Record video walkthroughs by phone or Loom.com
  • Add all instructions to company Drive OR Connectteam.com
  • Assign each workflow to a named owner

docs.title: Sales System Setup
docs.text:
Sources: - Playbook v2 EN — Ch. 10 - Playbook v1 EN — Sales System EPICs

Owner roles: Varia = CMO · Niklas = CFO · Mike = CTO

docs.title: Askel Tech Stack — Mandatory in All Portfolio Companies
docs.text: Category Tool Website / lead forms / CRM Wix.com SMS outreach / follow-ups Quriiri (quriiri.fi) Virtual assistant support Wannado (wannado.fi) Design work Upwork.com Prospect contact data Finder.fi Ops documentation / HR Connectteam.com Knowledge / Drive Google Workspace Daily ops tracking Trello DayToDay Ops board

docs.title: Sales System EPICs
docs.text: Goal: Build and launch the core systems, processes, and ownership structure needed to support scalable B2B and B2C customer acquisition.

docs.title: EPIC 1: Sales Preparation and System Setup (Owner: Mike / CTO)
docs.text:
Purpose: Build the commercial foundation needed to win and handle customers consistently.

B2B: - Train team on CRM usage - Define CRM workflow and pipeline stages - Create a proposal template with pricing

B2C: - Implement a 24/7 booking system: - AI call agent - Website booking - WhatsApp booking - Define fast credit check process for driver/customer approval

Success measures: CRM used by team weekly; proposal template in live use; at least one 24/7 booking channel live.

docs.title: EPIC 2: B2B Marketing System (Owner: Niklas / CFO)
docs.text:
Purpose: Create a repeatable outbound system for acquiring profitable business customers.

  • Build target contact lists (hotels, elderly care, emergency laundry/damage cases)
  • Conduct market research: margins by segment, priority segments
  • Launch outreach to contact database
  • Set up Wix automations: email + SMS/text
  • Build landing page with SEO

Success measures: Contact list completed; priority segments chosen; outreach launched; landing page published; automations active.

docs.title: EPIC 3: B2C Marketing System (Owner: Varia / CMO)
docs.text:
Purpose: Establish a minimum viable, repeatable local marketing engine.

  • Create a minimum viable marketing calendar (fridge magnets, flyers, newspaper, other local channels)
  • Build a repeatable task tracking system in Connecteam
  • Set up Wix email automations
  • Assign one responsible owner for B2C marketing execution
  • Define future scaling options; set budget and target channels

Success measures: Marketing calendar approved; owner assigned; recurring tasks tracked; budget and channels defined; first campaigns launched.

docs.title: EPIC 4: Invoicing (Owner: Niklas / CFO)
docs.text:
Purpose: Improve invoicing scalability, consistency, and operational efficiency.

  • Analyze Fivaldi API possibilities
  • Design and build an invoicing system prototype
  • Connect prototype to Fivaldi
  • Standardize invoicing code base
  • Test new invoicing system

Success measures: API feasibility assessed; prototype completed; testing completed; production rollout decision made.

docs.title: EPIC 5: Branding (Owner: Varia / CMO)
docs.text:
Purpose: Strengthen visibility, consistency, and trust across customer touchpoints.

  • Clarify portfolio company brand roles
  • Window branding (premises)
  • Vehicle branding
  • Website branding updates
  • Driver uniform/swag (T-shirts, hoodies)

Success measures: Core visual assets completed; at least 3 customer-facing branding touchpoints updated.

docs.title: EPIC 6: Ongoing Sales and Marketing Support
docs.text:
Purpose: Add capacity for repeatable execution without overloading internal management.

  • Identify a marketing partner for ongoing campaigns
  • Identify sales partner or in-house sales resource
  • Identify tasks that can be delegated to VAs
  • Build small Upwork team for repeatable support tasks
  • Explore AI agent support for repetitive admin/marketing tasks

Success measures: Resourcing model defined; at least one external/internal support resource activated; delegable task list created; repeatable tasks partially outsourced or automated.

docs.title: Mapping Key Role Workflows (Step 10.1)
docs.text:
• Create a workflow/process mapping system for every key role
  • Capture KPIs that measure role success
  • Record video walkthroughs by phone or Loom.com
  • Add all instructions to company Drive OR Connectteam.com
  • Assign each workflow to a named owner

docs.title: Success Criteria
docs.text: The acquisition is successful if ALL of the following are true by the milestone date: - ☐ Core commercial systems live and in active use - ☐ B2B and B2C acquisition through repeatable documented processes - ☐ Each key activity has a named owner - ☐ New customer acquisition tracked separately B2B and B2C - ☐ At least [X] B2B and [Y] B2C customers completed first paid order - ☐ New systems support scaling aligned with profitability targets

docs.title: Screening
docs.text:
Source: Playbook v2 EN — Ch. 4 + 5

Score thesis fit first using playbook-thesis. Run all four lenses before going to IC.

docs.title: 4.1 Thesis Fit Check (15 minutes — stop if any item fails)
docs.text:
• ☐ Company minimum 5 years old
  • ☐ Profit at least €100,000/year
  • ☐ Minimum 5 employees
  • ☐ Target sector (see playbook-thesis)
  • ☐ SWOT completed

docs.title: 4.2 Financial Screening
docs.text:
• ☐ 3 years of financial statements requested and received
  • ☐ Income statement + balance sheet in full view
  • ☐ Cost structure — top 3 largest costs identified (labour, fuel, rent, supplies)
  • ☐ Top 10 customers listed (B2B/B2C, recurring revenue flagged)
  • ☐ Monthly cash flow understood
  • ☐ Seasonal variation understood
  • ☐ Portfolio Company — Cost Structure Template completed

Cost Structure Template (Appendix B of v2 EN):

Line Item Year −2 (€) Year −1 (€) Latest Year (€) Notes Revenue COGS — Labour COGS — Materials / Supplies COGS — Fuel / Transport Gross Profit = Revenue − COGS Rent Utilities Marketing & Sales Admin / Software / IT Other Operating Costs EBITDA = Gross Profit − Overheads Gross Margin % = Gross Profit ÷ Revenue EBITDA Margin % = EBITDA ÷ Revenue

docs.title: 4.3 Market Research
docs.text:
• ☐ Top 3 local competitors identified (asiakastieto.fi)
  • ☐ Three international competitors identified for benchmarking
  • ☐ AI/tech adoption in sector understood
  • ☐ Customer register size known
  • ☐ Customer profile known (B2B vs B2C split)
  • ☐ Development Plan template completed

docs.title: 4.4 Resources — People & Assets
docs.text:
• ☐ In-person meeting scheduled — seller motivation understood
  • ☐ Psychological profile of seller completed (CPT Sheet — see below)
  • ☐ Team evaluated: roles, seller's tasks
  • ☐ Assets Inventory Template completed (see below)
  • ☐ Recruitment need and KPIs clear

Assets Inventory Template (Appendix C of v2 EN):

Asset Category Description / Make / Model Est. Value (€) Condition Included in Sale? Vehicle 1 ☐ Yes ☐ No Vehicle 2 ☐ Yes ☐ No Equipment / Machinery ☐ Yes ☐ No IT Systems / Software ☐ Yes ☐ No Customer Register Number of contacts: ___ ☐ Yes ☐ No Brand / Domain / Website ☐ Yes ☐ No Supplier / Customer Contracts Number: ___ ☐ Yes ☐ No Permits & Licences ☐ Yes ☐ No Other ☐ Yes ☐ No

docs.title: 4.5 Deal Terms
docs.text:
• ☐ How long seller stays post-sale confirmed
  • ☐ Seller's consulting terms post-employment confirmed
  • ☐ Customer register + marketing lists transfer confirmed
  • ☐ Business transfer or share transfer determined
  • ☐ Preliminary deal structure negotiated
  • ☐ Offer sent to seller
  • → Run the Valuation Risk Scoring Tool (playbook-valuation) before finalising any offer price.

docs.title: Chapter 5: Psychological Factors in Negotiation
docs.text:
All deals are made with emotions first. Understand the seller's emotional drivers — never challenge them, acknowledge and redirect.

Psychological Driver Matrix (CPT Sheet):

Psychological Driver Behaviour Underlying Fear What They Need to Hear Identity fusion Mixing personal worth with company value "I'll be forgotten." "Your legacy continues through us." Control preservation Itemising, micromanaging the deal "I'm powerless." "We'll handle it your way where possible." Sunk-cost emotion Overvaluing old projects "My effort was wasted." "You laid the groundwork — we're finishing it." Mortality salience Indecision, nostalgia "This is the end." "This is a continuation, not a closure." Need for fairness Anchoring on numbers "They're tricking me." "We want this fair for both sides."

Complete this table for the primary seller before IC. Copy for each deal.

docs.title: Sourcing
docs.text:
Source: Playbook v2 EN — Ch. 3

Score all leads against the thesis in the playbook-thesis skill before creating cards.

docs.title: Channels
docs.text: Three channels: broker listings, public data weak signals, direct outreach.

docs.title: Broker Platforms (Finland)
docs.text:
• yritysporssi.fi — primary Finnish business-for-sale marketplace
  • yrityskaupat.net — secondary Finnish listing platform
  • firmakauppa.fi — additional Finnish listings
  • Broker list (Välittäjät): Google Sheets

docs.title: Broker Platforms (Sweden)
docs.text:
• bolagsplatsen.se — primary marketplace (~890 listings, confirmed live 2026-04-21)
  • exitpartner.se — active broker network, NDA-gated
  • företagsförmedlingen.se — live homepage; listing URLs intermittently broken

docs.title: Weak-Signal Sourcing
docs.text:
Finding unlisted businesses via Finder.fi + public records. Signals: - Owner age 55+ — strongest single predictor of exit-readiness - Revenue declining 2–3 years but still profitable - Stale or absent web presence - Not listed for sale but fits all thesis criteria

Thresholds: 3+ signals → investigate; 5+ signals → outreach-worthy.

docs.title: Owner Age — How to Estimate
docs.text:
Step 1 — Public records: - PRH / YTJ sometimes includes birth year (format: DDMMYY in henkilötunnus prefix) - asiakastieto.fi päättäjät page occasionally shows birth year - proff.fi board listings sometimes include age

Step 2 — LinkedIn / social media: - Graduation years anchor an age estimate ("Class of 198X" = born early-to-mid 1960s)

Step 3 — Photo estimation: - Company website, local news, LinkedIn photo, Facebook - Note estimated range (e.g. "appears 60+") — do not overstate precision

Step 4 — Company history inference: - Founded 1985 + founder still running = likely 60s+

Record as: **Omistajan ikä-arvio:** ~6X (syntymävuosi [XXXX tai ei tiedossa], lähde: [PRH / LinkedIn / kuva / arvio])

Scoring: age 55–59 = 1 exit signal; 60–64 = 2; 65+ = 3.

docs.title: finder.fi Workflow
docs.text:
• Use browser-capable tooling when JS rendering is needed.
  • Do not trust first-pass secondary-source revenue estimates when finder.fi verification is available.
  • Check revenue history, margins, headcount, founding date, board/management details.
  • For non-obvious companies, search finder.fi first.

docs.title: Tier 1
docs.text: • yritysporssi.fi, yrityskaupat.net, finder.fi

docs.title: Tier 2
docs.text: • kauppalehti.fi / proff.fi, ytj.fi / PRH, Suomen Yritysmyynti / Finlandia Business

docs.title: Tier 3
docs.text: • Hilma, industry-specific registries, industry associations, direct outreach

docs.title: Referral Networks (underdeveloped — Varia, 2026-04-02)
docs.text:
• Suomen Yrittäjät — 100k+ member SME association; local chapters have direct retiring-owner relationships
  • Omistajanvaihdos RY — explicitly focused on business succession; best single partner for warm deal flow
  • Accounting firms (tilitoimistot) — see the P&L before any broker does

Approach: offer co-referral, not just awareness.

docs.title: Follow-on Sourcing
docs.text:
Read knowledge/sourcing/portfolio.yaml before every sweep. For each existing holding: - Search adjacent/overlapping regions in the same sector - Tag matching leads as follow-on / add-on candidates - Reference the parent holding explicitly

Thesis flexibility for add-ons: normal thesis filters can be relaxed for credible add-ons. Flag with "Add On Acquisition" Trello label.

Current holdings: Melers Oy — laundry/textile services, Turku area.

docs.title: Trello Card Standards
docs.text:
Always clone the template card (📋 TEMPLATE: PROSPEKTI) on the Case Scouting board — never create from scratch.

Every prospect card must include: - Y-tunnus (verified in PRH/finder.fi) - Source link - CEO: name, LinkedIn, contact details - Chairman / owner: name, LinkedIn, contact details, age estimate - Financial analysis link: https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1llwFP6A7K8D7KaMK9hUOZrz_Bh69od0q/edit (paste in "Kopioi ja täytä talousanalyysi" checklist item)

Description template:

## MYYJÄN YHTEYSTIEDOT:
[contact info or "Ei tiedossa — ei myytävänä"]

---

**Y-tunnus:** [xxx]
**Sijainti:** [city, region]
**Toimiala:** [sector]
**Liikevaihto:** [Xk (year)]
**Liikevoitto:** [Xk (X%)]
**Henkilöstö:** [~N]
**Perustettu:** [year (~age)]

**Toimitusjohtaja (CEO):**
- Nimi: [name]
- LinkedIn: [url or "ei löydy"]
- Yhteystiedot: [email/phone or "ei tiedossa"]

**Omistaja / hallituksen puheenjohtaja:**
- Nimi: [name]
- LinkedIn: [url or "ei löydy"]
- Yhteystiedot: [email/phone or "ei tiedossa"]
- Ikä-arvio: [~XX tai "ei tiedossa"] (lähde: [PRH / LinkedIn / kuva / arvio])

**Miksi kiinnostava:**
- [reason]

**Signaalit:** [sale status / outreach note]
**Lähde:** [sweep name or source URL]

yrityspörssi.fi specifics: - Broker-gated, anonymized listings — the site blocks direct scraping - When a team member shares a listing URL, parse what's visible in the slug - Always check Case Scouting board for duplicates before adding - Never add anonymized prospects without the listing URL and a "broker contact required" note

Before adding any card: 1. Verify Y-tunnus exists (PRH API or finder.fi) 2. Confirm revenue/profit data matches thesis 3. Add source link, CEO, and owner/chairman info

docs.title: Anti-patterns
docs.text:
• Do not reverse-engineer anonymous listings when contacting the broker is simpler.
  • Do not rely on finder.fi location filters; filter client-side.
  • Do not treat weak sentiment signals as a substitute for financial verification.
  • Do not assume English-language search is useful for Finnish SME sourcing.
  • Do not add prospects without Y-tunnus and source link.

docs.title: After Each Session
docs.text: Write or update: session thesis, session notes, lead files, debrief (if new methods or failures surfaced). Archive in knowledge/sourcing/.

docs.title: Acquisition Thesis
docs.text: Source: Playbook v2 EN — Ch. 2 (canonical). Last synced 2026-05-13.

docs.title: Criteria — ALL must be met
docs.text: Criterion Target Annual Revenue €400K – €1.5M Annual Profit (EBITDA) €100K+ (adjusted — see below) Asking Price Max €1M Company Age Minimum 10 years (LLC / Oy) Employees Minimum 5 Geography Finland, Sweden, Estonia Entity type Osakeyhtiö (Oy) or equivalent

docs.title: Target Sectors
docs.text:
• Cleaning & laundry services (siivouspalvelu, pesula, pesutupa)
  • Property maintenance (kiinteistöhuolto, LVI, putkiasennus, sähköasennus, nuohous)
  • Waste & recycling collection (jätehuolto)
  • Funeral services (hautaustoimisto)
  • Transport & logistics (kuljetus)
  • Tyre services (rengasliike)
  • Auto repair (autokorjaamo)
  • Security (vartiointi)
  • Painting (maalausliike)

docs.title: Immediate Disqualifiers
docs.text:
• ✗ Business less than 5 years old
  • ✗ No recurring revenue
  • ✗ No credible exit in 3–5 years
  • ✗ Cannot be operated at max 5 hrs/week post-optimisation
  • ✗ Cannot generate min €100K capital gain per owner on exit

docs.title: Sector Ratings (from completed sweeps)
docs.text: Sector Rating Sweet Spot Density Consolidation Exit Property maintenance 8/10 High (11,500 cos) High (Alltime, Tapiolan Lämpö) Funeral homes 8/10 Moderate (~500 cos) Low (Memoria network) Waste collection 7/10 Low (barbell) High (L&T/Luotea) Industrial cleaning 7/10 Low (~300 cos) High (L&T, ISS, Alltime)

docs.title: Ideal Seller Profile
docs.text:
• Founder aged 55–70, approaching retirement
  • No clear successor
  • Business 15–40 years old
  • Owner salary compressed into the business
  • Located outside Helsinki metro (preferred, not required)

docs.title: Adjusted EBITDA — The Melers Pattern
docs.text:
Many thesis-grade businesses look less profitable than they are. Watch for: - Owner pays themselves a modest salary but works full-time - Equipment depreciation is aggressive - Personal expenses run through the business

Always calculate: Adjusted EBITDA = reported profit + owner salary + excess depreciation + personal expenses

The reported number is almost never the real number. Always adjust before scoring.

docs.title: Geography Notes
docs.text:
v2 EN (canonical) sets geography as Finland + Sweden + Estonia.

  • Finland — primary market; all sourcing crons cover this.
  • Sweden — covered by cron-swedish-sweep (bolagsplatsen.se and others).
  • Estonia — in thesis but no dedicated sourcing cron yet. Flag Estonian opportunities ad-hoc when encountered via cron-swedish-sweep or other channels. A dedicated cron can be added once we have a reliable Estonian source.

docs.title: Known Constraints
docs.text:
• The barbell problem: the €400K–1.5M sweet spot is thinner than expected.
  • Financial verification is non-negotiable: always verify via finder.fi when available.
  • High exit-readiness + weak financials = distressed, not hidden gem.
  • Follow-on flexibility: if a company is a credible add-on to an existing Askel holding (e.g. Melers Oy), normal thesis filters can be relaxed. Flag with the "Add On Acquisition" Trello label.

docs.title: Valuation Risk Scoring Tool
docs.text:
Source: Playbook v2 EN — Appendix G

Formula: Business Value = Annual Cash Flow ÷ Yield Rate

Each ticked item = 1 point. Higher score = lower risk = lower yield rate.

docs.title: 1. Financial Performance
docs.text:
• ☐ Revenue growing year over year
  • ☐ Gross margins are stable and healthy
  • ☐ Majority of revenue is recurring (contracts / standing orders)
  • ☐ Cash flow is regular and predictable
  • ☐ Debt levels are low and manageable
  • ☐ Revenue is not significantly seasonal

docs.title: 2. Market Position
docs.text:
• ☐ Target market is large and growing
  • ☐ Clear competitive advantage / unique selling proposition
  • ☐ High barriers to entry for competitors
  • ☐ Strong customer loyalty and retention
  • ☐ Brand is well known in the market
  • ☐ Favourable position vs. competitors (price, quality, or niche leader)

docs.title: 3. Operational Strength
docs.text:
• ☐ Business can operate without the owner's daily involvement
  • ☐ Skilled, reliable, and loyal staff
  • ☐ Efficient processes with minimal waste
  • ☐ Operational software and automation in use (CRM, scheduling, invoicing, etc.)
  • ☐ Documented work processes
  • ☐ Consistent quality control system
  • ☐ Business model is scalable without large cost spikes
  • ☐ Reliable and diversified suppliers

docs.title: 4. Intangible Factors
docs.text:
• ☐ Strong brand reputation
  • ☐ Google rating above 4.0 with at least 30 reviews
  • ☐ Intellectual property protected (trademarks, patents, copyrights)
  • ☐ Exclusive supplier or customer agreements
  • ☐ Proprietary technology or unique processes
  • ☐ Long-term customer and supplier contracts
  • ☐ Strong relationships with industry players and partners

docs.title: 5. Risk Factors
docs.text:
• ☐ No single customer represents more than 20% of revenue
  • ☐ Industry is stable and not highly cyclical
  • ☐ Full regulatory and permit compliance
  • ☐ No active litigation or disputes
  • ☐ Revenue is not tied to a single geographic area
  • ☐ No 'key person risk' (one person leaving does not paralyse operations)
  • ☐ Contingency or crisis management plan in place

docs.title: Score → Yield Rate Table
docs.text: Score Risk Profile Yield Rate What it means 29–34 Low Risk 20% Stable business, solid systems, predictable results. Easy to take over. 20–28 Moderate Risk 25% Some gaps, but generally strong. Buyer will need to make improvements. 10–19 High Risk 28% Multiple operational or financial weaknesses; higher return requirement. 0–9 Very High Risk 30%+ Significant gaps; owner-dependent; results hard to predict.

docs.title: Example Calculation
docs.text:
If annual cash flow = €74,500 and risk profile yields 28%:

Value = €74,500 ÷ 0.28 ≈ €266,071

Why it works: Higher risk means the buyer demands a greater return on every euro invested. The same cash flow must cover a higher return requirement — which reduces the business's value.

Note: Always use adjusted cash flow (see playbook-thesis — the Melers Pattern). Reported profit is almost never the right input to this formula.

docs.title: The Askel Playbook
docs.text: Source: Playbook v2 EN — Ch. 1

docs.title: Investment Philosophies
docs.text:
1. Boring is beautiful — predictable cash flows, low tech risk, high operational leverage.
  2. We build under market prices. Disciplined valuation is non-negotiable.
  3. Operational excellence compounds. A well-run laundromat beats a poorly run tech startup.
  4. Speed of learning matters more than perfect information.
  5. We treat sellers with respect. Every deal has a human on the other side.

docs.title: Acquisition Stages
docs.text:
Stage 1: Sourcing   →   Stage 2: Screening   →   Stage 3: IC   →   Stage 4: DD   →   Stage 5: Closing   →   Stage 6: Knowledge Transfer

Each stage has defined entry criteria, an output, and a handoff to the next. A deal that fails any kill question is dropped — no sunk cost fallacy.

docs.title: Sub-skill Routing
docs.text:
Skill Trigger condition playbook-thesis Scoring a lead, pass/fail on thesis criteria, any thesis question playbook-sourcing Any sourcing sweep, lead discovery, weak-signal scan playbook-screening First pass on a lead that passed thesis; before IC playbook-valuation Any valuation question, yield rate, Business Value calculation playbook-ic Preparing for IC, running the IC meeting, post-IC steps playbook-dd Any due diligence task, dataroom, DD1/DD2/DD3 playbook-knowledge-transfer Post-acquisition transition, handover, team onboarding playbook-sales-system Post-acquisition sales system setup, CRM, B2B/B2C outreach

Progressive disclosure: load the most specific sub-skill that covers the question. Never load the whole playbook when one chapter is enough.
